items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Willie Burton. Minnesota G was drafted 9th by MIA in 90. Crowded backcourt left him as bench guy & struggled w/depression. Waived in 94, played w/PHI & had career high 15 ppg (and a 50 point game). Also played w/SA & CHA thru 99. Getting psych PHD now.

items.title: Dating back to Game 3 of the Sixers series, Landry Shamet is shooting:
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Dating back to Game 3 of the Sixers series, Landry Shamet is shooting:
73% from the floor (21-of-29) and 
81% from three-point range (17-of-21)

That’s an effective FG% of 1.017%

Is that good? That seems good.

items.title: The 3-point brick vs the Knicks this postseason has been something else
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
The 3-point brick vs the Knicks this postseason has been something else

Hawks: 31.2% (ranked 5th in regular season, 37.1%)

76ers: 31.3% (34.9% 3s, ranked 23rd)

Cavaliers: 28.9% (36% 3s, ranked 13th)

Spurs: 35.9% 3s (ranked 15th)
Thunder: 36.5% 3s (ranked 9th)

items.title: The New York Knicks have been a National Basketball Association team from the beginning of the damn league
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
The New York Knicks have been a National Basketball Association team from the beginning of the damn league

Their three largest playoff series margins of victory have been this year

+105 vs Hawks
+89 vs 76ers
+77 vs Cavaliers

previous high was +54 in 1972 East semis vs Bullets.

items.title: Knicks are the first team in NBA history to have three 20+ point series clinching wins in a single playoff run.
items.author: Keerthika Uthayakumar
items.body_excerpt:
Knicks are the first team in NBA history to have three 20+ point series clinching wins in a single playoff run. 

51-point win in Game 6 vs the Hawks
30-point win in Game 4 vs the 76ers
37-point win in Game 4 vs the Cavs

items.title: 1) Morey’s quote is the reason why it’s a repeated story and that was an unforced error
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
1) Morey’s quote is the reason why it’s a repeated story and that was an unforced error

2) The Sixers got a totally reasonable trade return for Jared McCain.

3) It was a good trade for the Thunder, especially given their overflowing cupboard of picks.

All can be true at once.

items.title: May 22, 1985: A Larry Bird steal with five seconds to go seals Boston's 102-100 win over the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 5 of the Eastern Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 22, 1985: A Larry Bird steal with five seconds to go seals Boston's 102-100 win over the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 5 of the Eastern Conference Finals.

Bird scored 17 PTS and Dennis Johnson had 23 PTS/8 AST for the Celtics, who advanced to face the Lakers in the NBA Finals.

items.title: May 22, 1985: A Larry Bird steal with five seconds to go seals Boston's 102-100 win over the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 5 of the Eastern Conference Finals.

items.title: May 22, 1977: Philadelphia’s Julius Erving dunks on Portland’s Bill Walton during Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 22, 1977: Philadelphia’s Julius Erving dunks on Portland’s Bill Walton during Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Dr. J scored a game-high 33 PTS (14-24 FG) and Doug Collins had 30 PTS/6 AST/6 REB in the 76ers’ 107-101 win. Walton led the Trail Blazers with 28 PTS (11-17 FG)/20 REB.

New: Reviewing Paul George’s second season with the Sixers, a wildly mixed bag.

After returning from suspension, George finally proved capable of submitting star-level scoring nights. But the playoffs exposed the major flaw which remains in his shot diet:
www.phillyvoice.com/sixers-news-...

items.title: May 22, 1983: Philadelphia's Julius Erving dunks on Magic Johnson during Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 22, 1983: Philadelphia's Julius Erving dunks on Magic Johnson during Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Dr. J had 20 PTS/10 REB/9 AST/5 BLK and Moses Malone had 27 PTS/18 REB in the 76ers’ 113-107 win. Magic had 19 PTS/11 AST/9 REB and Norm Nixon scored 26 PTS for the Lakers.
